cards.back: A subfield of machine learning that uses neural networks to analyze and interpret data.
An area of machine learning where an agent learns to take actions to maximize a reward.

cards.front: What is the main difference between Supervised and Unsupervised Learning?
cards.back: Supervised Learning: The model is trained on labeled data, while Unsupervised Learning: The model is trained on unlabeled data.

cards.front: What is the primary difference between Feedforward Networks and Recurrent Networks?
cards.back: Feedforward Networks: The data flows only in one direction, while Recurrent Networks: The data flows in a loop, allowing the model to keep track of state.

cards.front: What is the purpose of Batch Normalization?
cards.back: Batch Normalization: A technique used to normalize the input data for each layer, improving the stability and performance of the model.
